task:
Add an inline 'Launch fix' button to each ticket CARD in the Tickets tab, mirroring PriorityRow's inline Launch on priority cards. Founder-requested (run_133): today launching a ticket fix is only possible inside the ticket detail modal; the founder wants a card-level Launch so a fix can be launched straight from the Tickets list without opening the modal.

SURFACE: packages/ui/app/sections/dashboard/Dashboard.tsx — the TicketsTab component (currently lines ~87-153). Only this file should change for the feature; you may update the dashboard test that asserts ticket-card structure if needed.

WHAT TO DO:
- In TicketsTab, each open ticket renders as a clickable <button> card (the whole card opens the detail Modal via setSelectedId). Add an inline Launch control onto the card, alongside the id/title/arrow row, that triggers a launch directly.
- Mirror PriorityRow's inline Launch exactly (Priorities.tsx:53): `<Button variant="secondary" size="sm" icon="play" disabled={launchDisabled} title={launchTitle} onClick={(e) => { e.stopPropagation(); onLaunchTicket(ticket) }}>Launch</Button>`.
- REUSE the existing wiring already present in TicketsTab: `launchDisabled` (= !live || launchBlocked, line 91), `launchTitle` (line 92), and the `onLaunchTicket` prop. Do NOT add a new prop, a new launch path, or a parallel launch handler — single source of truth.
- `e.stopPropagation()` is REQUIRED on the card-level Launch onClick so clicking Launch does NOT also open the detail modal.
- The card's outer element is currently a <button>; nesting an interactive <Button> inside a <button> is invalid HTML. Restructure the card's outer element to a clickable <div> (role/keyboard affordance as the existing PriorityRow card uses — match how Priorities.tsx structures its clickable card with an inner Launch button) so the inline Button is valid, while preserving the existing click-to-open-modal behavior, hover styles, and layout. Match the surrounding style conventions; keep visual change minimal (just the added inline Launch on the right of each card).
- Keep the in-modal 'Launch fix' button (modal footer, line 139) exactly as-is — both entry points stay, both call the same onLaunchTicket path.

MUST NOT BREAK: the existing card → detail modal open behavior; the in-modal Launch fix button; the empty-state ('No open tickets'); Priorities and Runs tabs (do not touch them); the launchDisabled/launchTitle guard semantics (offline + run-in-progress).

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
1. Each open ticket card shows an inline 'Launch' button that calls onLaunchTicket(ticket) and is disabled with the correct title when launchDisabled is true.
2. Clicking the inline Launch does NOT open the detail modal (stopPropagation verified).
3. Clicking elsewhere on the card still opens the detail modal.
4. No new props/handlers added; reuses onLaunchTicket + launchDisabled + launchTitle.
5. `pnpm --dir '/Volumes/NAS LOCAL/CoCoder' --filter @cocoder/ui typecheck` passes (or the repo's UI typecheck command) and the dashboard test suite passes (`pnpm --dir '/Volumes/NAS LOCAL/CoCoder' --filter @cocoder/ui test` or the dashboard test file). Update test assertions only where they pin the old card structure; do not weaken coverage.

Report the exact files changed, the diff of TicketsTab, and the test/typecheck command output.

Add a persisted TICKET ORDER mechanism by GENERALIZING the existing priority-order mechanism — do NOT add a parallel mechanism (single source of truth, global #7). This is the backend/primitive half of 'reorderable tickets'; the UI drag-reorder is a separate follow-on atom, so DO NOT touch UI in this atom.

KEY FILE: packages/daemon/src/priority-order.ts already owns BOTH `readPriorities` (which applies a `order.json` manifest via `readManifest` + dedup/append-unseen) AND the `readTickets` wrapper around @cocoder/core. Generalize WITHIN this file: extract the manifest read/apply/write logic that `readPriorities`/`writePriorityOrder` use into shared internal helpers, and reuse them for tickets. The core reader (packages/core/src/tickets/loader.ts `readTickets`) MUST stay a pure reader — apply ordering only in the daemon layer, exactly as priorities do.

WHAT TO BUILD:
1. Ticket order file at `cocoder/tickets/order.json` (a JSON array of ticket ids), parallel to priorities' `cocoder/priorities/order.json`.
2. The daemon `readTickets(ticketsDir)` wrapper (priority-order.ts:63) must apply that manifest to the OPEN tickets only: reorder the `state === 'open'` tickets per the manifest using the SAME dedup/append-unseen/tolerate-unknown-and-missing logic as `readPriorities` (ids in manifest but not present are skipped; open tickets not in the manifest keep their current id-sorted order, appended after the manifest-ordered ones). CLOSED tickets are unaffected and stay after the open ones, exactly as today. No manifest file → return tickets exactly as today (no behavior change).
3. Add `writeTicketOrder(ticketsDir, requestedOrder: readonly string[]): Promise<string[]>` mirroring `writePriorityOrder`: filter requestedOrder to valid OPEN ticket ids, atomic write via a `.order.json.tmp` + rename, return the written order.
4. Endpoint `POST /workspaces/:id/tickets/reorder` in packages/daemon/src/routes.ts mirroring the existing `POST /workspaces/:id/priorities/reorder` route + `reorderPriorities` handler: parse with the existing generic `reorderBody` (reuse it — it's already `{order: string[]}`); 400 on invalid body; add a `reorderTickets` handler that 404s on unknown workspace, calls `writeTicketOrder(ticketsDir(ws.path), order)`, commits the `cocoder/tickets/order.json` path via `commitGovernance` with message `governance: reorder tickets (<workspaceId>)`, appends an audit with action `ticket-reorder`, honors `governanceCommitFailed`, and returns `{order, committedSha}`. Add a `ticketOrderFile(workspacePath)` helper next to `priorityOrderFile`.

MUST REUSE / NOT FORK: the manifest read+apply+write logic must be ONE shared implementation used by both priorities and tickets (extract helpers; do not copy-paste a second copy). Reuse `reorderBody`, `commitGovernance`, `appendAudit`, `governanceCommitFailed`, `findWorkspace`, `ticketsDir`. Commit ONLY through the ADR-0023 spine via `commitGovernance` — introduce no new commit lane.

MUST NOT BREAK: priority reorder behavior and its tests (byte-for-byte API); core `readTickets` purity and its tests; the GET /tickets and POST /tickets endpoints; closed-ticket ordering.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
1. With no `cocoder/tickets/order.json`, GET tickets is unchanged (open id-sorted, then closed).
2. With a manifest, open tickets follow it; unknown ids skipped; un-manifested open tickets appended in id order; closed tickets unaffected.
3. `POST /workspaces/:id/tickets/reorder {order:[...]}` writes the file, commits via the spine, audits `ticket-reorder`, returns `{order, committedSha}`; 404 unknown ws; 400 bad body.
4. The manifest logic is shared (one implementation), not duplicated.
5. Add/extend daemon tests (mirror the priority-reorder cases in packages/daemon/tests/mutations.test.ts and read-surfaces.test.ts) covering: order applied to open tickets, unknown/missing ids tolerated, closed unaffected, the reorder endpoint commit+audit+return shape. Do not weaken existing assertions.
6. `pnpm --dir '/Volumes/NAS LOCAL/CoCoder' --filter @cocoder/daemon typecheck` and `pnpm --dir '/Volumes/NAS LOCAL/CoCoder' --filter @cocoder/daemon test` pass (plus @cocoder/core test if you touched core — you should not need to).

Report the files changed, the diffs for priority-order.ts and routes.ts, and the typecheck/test output.

Add DRAG-REORDER for tickets in the Tickets tab, mirroring the existing priorities drag-reorder path END-TO-END. Do NOT invent a new mechanism — mirror priorities at every seam. The daemon backend already exists (atom 1: GET /tickets returns open tickets pre-ordered by cocoder/tickets/order.json; POST /workspaces/:id/tickets/reorder writes+commits it). This atom wires the UI + IPC client to that endpoint and adds the drag interaction.

The priorities reorder path to MIRROR, seam by seam:
- packages/ui/electron/ipc-contract.ts: `CHANNELS.prioritiesReorder: 'oz:priorities:reorder'` (line ~17) and the `OzApi.prioritiesReorder(workspaceId, order): Promise<readonly string[]>` method (line ~261).
- packages/ui/electron/preload.ts: `prioritiesReorder: (ws, order) => ipcRenderer.invoke(CHANNELS.prioritiesReorder, ws, order)` (line ~22).
- packages/ui/electron/main.ts: `ipcMain.handle(CHANNELS.prioritiesReorder, (_e, ws, order) => reorderPrioritiesViaDaemon(ws, order))` (line ~32). Find `reorderPrioritiesViaDaemon` (the main-process fn that POSTs to the daemon priorities/reorder endpoint) and add a parallel `reorderTicketsViaDaemon` that POSTs to `/workspaces/:id/tickets/reorder` the same way.
- packages/ui/app/live.ts: `persistOrder(oz, wsId, ids)` (line ~190) calls `oz.prioritiesReorder`. Add a parallel `persistTicketOrder(oz, wsId, ids)` calling `oz.ticketsReorder` with the same best-effort try/catch.
- packages/ui/app/App.tsx: the `reorder(from, to)` handler (line ~317) reorders the local priorities list and calls `persistOrder` when live; it is passed as `onReorder={reorder}` to Dashboard (line ~644). Tickets are held in `ticketsByWs` / `const tickets = ticketsByWs[activeId]` (lines ~82, ~266).
- packages/ui/app/sections/dashboard/Priorities.tsx: `PriorityRow` drag handlers (draggable + onDragStart/onDragOver/onDragEnd/onDrop calling `onDrag(type,index)`, with a `didDrag` useRef to suppress the click-open after a drag — lines 17,24-25) and `PrioritiesPanel`'s `drag` state + `handleDrag` (lines ~117-127) calling `onReorder(from,to)` on drop.

WHAT TO BUILD (mirror exactly):
1. ipc-contract.ts: add `ticketsReorder: 'oz:tickets:reorder'` to CHANNELS and `ticketsReorder(workspaceId, order): Promise<readonly string[]>` to the OzApi interface.
2. preload.ts: add the `ticketsReorder` bridge line.
3. main.ts: add `reorderTicketsViaDaemon` (mirror reorderPrioritiesViaDaemon, hitting the tickets/reorder endpoint) and register `ipcMain.handle(CHANNELS.ticketsReorder, ...)`.
4. live.ts: add `persistTicketOrder(oz, wsId, ids)` mirroring persistOrder.
5. App.tsx: add a `reorderTickets(from, to)` handler. The from/to indices are positions within the OPEN-tickets list (that is all TicketsTab renders). It must: take the current `ticketsByWs[activeId]`, split open vs closed (open = state==='open', preserving their order), move the open item from->to, recombine `[...reorderedOpen, ...closed]`, `setTicketsByWs` optimistically, and when `live` call `persistTicketOrder(oz, activeId, reorderedOpen.map(t => t.id))`. Thread it to Dashboard as a new `onReorderTickets` prop (add to the Dashboard props type + destructure + pass to TicketsTab).
6. Dashboard.tsx TicketsTab: add `onReorderTickets: (from: number, to: number) => void` to its props. Add a `drag` state + `handleDrag` mirroring PrioritiesPanel (start/over/drop/end; on drop call `onReorderTickets(drag.from, index)` when from!==null && from!==index). Make each open-ticket card draggable mirroring PriorityRow: `draggable`, onDragStart/onDragOver(preventDefault)/onDragEnd/onDrop(preventDefault) calling handleDrag(type,index); use a `didDrag` ref so a drag does not also fire the card's click-to-open-modal; add the drop-target highlight (boxShadow) and dragging opacity like PriorityRow; cursor 'grab'. A drag handle icon (`dots-six-vertical`) is a nice-to-have matching PriorityRow but optional.

MUST PRESERVE (from atom 0, do not regress): the inline 'Launch' button on each card (with e.stopPropagation so neither launch nor drag opens the modal), the card click → detail modal, the in-modal 'Launch fix' button, the launchDisabled/launchTitle guards, the empty state, and the Priorities + Runs tabs (do not touch their behavior). Priorities reorder must remain byte-for-byte unchanged.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
1. Dragging an open ticket card to a new position calls onReorderTickets, optimistically reorders the rendered open list, and (when live) POSTs the new open-id order via oz.ticketsReorder → daemon tickets/reorder.
2. A drag does NOT open the detail modal; a plain click still does; the inline Launch still launches without opening the modal.
3. New IPC seam mirrors prioritiesReorder at all four electron layers (channel, OzApi type, preload, main handler) + live.ts client.
4. Priorities reorder unchanged.
5. Update the OzApi test mock in packages/ui/tests/live-app.test.tsx (it stubs `prioritiesReorder` at line ~155) to also stub `ticketsReorder`, and add a dashboard test asserting a ticket drag triggers onReorderTickets with the right indices and does not open the modal. Do not weaken existing assertions.
6. `pnpm --dir '/Volumes/NAS LOCAL/CoCoder' typecheck` (root) passes, and `pnpm --dir '/Volumes/NAS LOCAL/CoCoder' --filter @cocoder/ui test` passes.

Report files changed, the TicketsTab + App.tsx + ipc diffs, and the typecheck/test output.

RE-SCOPE of the ticket drag-reorder atom (it was rejected at verify for ONE defect — fix only that; keep everything else you already built in the working tree). The IPC layers, persistTicketOrder, the App reorderTickets handler, the TicketsTab drag wiring, and the tests are all correct and should remain. The whole atom must land once this fix is in.

DEFECT TO FIX (packages/ui/app/sections/dashboard/Dashboard.tsx, TicketsTab): the suppression flag is a single SHARED boolean ref `didDrag` used by ALL ticket cards — armed `true` on every onDragStart and only cleared inside onClick. Because it is shared, after ANY drag-reorder the next click on ANY ticket card is swallowed (the detail modal won't open until a second click). PriorityRow avoids this because each row is its own component with its own ref; this map shares one ref across all cards.

FIX: make the suppression INDEX-KEYED so it isolates to the dragged card, matching PriorityRow's per-row behavior. Change `didDrag` from a boolean ref to a ref holding the dragged index (e.g. `const draggedIndex = useRef<number | null>(null)`). On `onDragStart` set `draggedIndex.current = index` (in addition to the existing handleDrag('start', index)). In the card's `onClick`, replace the boolean check with: `if (draggedIndex.current === index) { draggedIndex.current = null; return } setSelectedId(ticket.id)`. This way only the dragged card's immediately-following click is suppressed; a click on any OTHER card opens its modal on the first click.

DO NOT change: the IPC seam (ipc-contract/preload/main/priorities-sync reorderTicketsViaDaemon), live.ts persistTicketOrder, App.tsx reorderTickets, the draggable handlers / drop-target highlight / opacity / grab cursor / drag-handle icon, the inline Launch button (with stopPropagation), the card→modal open, the in-modal Launch, the empty state, or the Priorities/Runs tabs.

TESTS: the existing dashboard test (`drags ticket cards to reorder without opening the detail modal`) clicks the DRAGGED card after the drop and asserts its modal stays closed — that must still pass with the index-keyed ref (draggedIndex===that index → suppressed). Add ONE assertion (or a small new test) proving the cross-card case the old code broke: after a drag of card A, a FIRST click on a DIFFERENT card B opens B's detail modal immediately (no swallowed click). Keep the live-app ticketsReorder test as-is. Do not weaken any assertions.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
1. Suppression is index-keyed: dragging a card suppresses only that card's next click; a first click on any other card opens its modal.
2. Drag still reorders + persists via oz.ticketsReorder (unchanged); drag does not open the dragged card's modal; inline Launch still works without opening the modal; priorities reorder unchanged.
3. A test proves the cross-card first-click-opens behavior.
4. `pnpm --dir '/Volumes/NAS LOCAL/CoCoder' typecheck` (root) and `pnpm --dir '/Volumes/NAS LOCAL/CoCoder' --filter @cocoder/ui test` both pass.

Report the TicketsTab diff (the ref change + onClick) and the test addition, plus typecheck/test output.

Add a `create-ticket` AUTHORING PLAY runnable by ALL personas (Oz/Oscar/Bob/Deb), so any persona can file a ticket mid-run — the agent-facing entry that lands through the SAME ticket writer as the founder-facing dashboard modal (POST /workspaces/:id/tickets). This is the last in-scope build item for the tickets-review priority. Mirror the existing `create-priority` authoring Play exactly; do NOT invent a parallel ticket-write path (single owner per surface, global #7).

BACKGROUND (read these first):
- packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts:47 `AUTHORING_PLAY_IDS = ['create-priority','edit-priority','archive-priority']` and `requestAuthoringPlay` (~line 884): an authoring Play loads packages/personas/base/plays/<id>.md, runs it headless on the assigned model, and commits its writeScope through the ONE governance spine (commitGovernance). The Play body is prose the agent executes; it writes files directly and must NOT run git.
- packages/personas/base/plays/create-priority.md is the template to mirror (frontmatter id/label/kind: headless/writeScope; body = validate → refuse on collision → compose via the route's named compose fn → write → round-trip-validate → do not commit).
- packages/daemon/src/routes.ts `createTicket` (~line 614) is the founder-modal writer: `nextTicketId(dir)` → `slugifyTitle(title)` → `composeTicketMarkdown(id, input, created)` (private in routes.ts; templates YAML frontmatter id/title/type/status/priority/owner/created) → write `cocoder/tickets/open/NNNN-slug.md` → INDEX Open-table row `| [id](./open/file) | ticketTableCell(title) | type | ticketTableCell(priority) | TICKET_OWNER |` via the core `insertOpenTicketIndexRow`/`ticketTableCell`/`readTicketIndex` helpers → commit [ticketRel, indexRel] via spine. `todayIso` gives `created`. Defaults: type in {bug,task,question,spike} default task; priority default none; owner = TICKET_OWNER.
- Ticket 0015: tickets with missing/invalid frontmatter were SILENTLY DROPPED by the loader. The create-ticket Play MUST template proper frontmatter so this cannot happen, and MUST validate the written ticket round-trips.

DO THIS:
1. SINGLE-OWNER EXTRACTION: move the ticket FILE-FORMAT owner out of the daemon route into @cocoder/core so both the modal and the Play land through the same writer. Extract `composeTicketMarkdown` (and the `TICKET_OWNER` constant, and `slugifyTitle` IF it is ticket-specific — if slugifyTitle is shared with priorities, leave it where it is and have the route keep passing the slug) into packages/core/src/tickets/ (e.g. a new compose.ts), export it from packages/core/src/tickets/index.ts, and refactor routes.ts `createTicket` to import and use it. This MUST be behavior-preserving (byte-identical ticket markdown + INDEX row); the existing daemon tickets-create test must stay green to prove it.
2. Add `'create-ticket'` to `AUTHORING_PLAY_IDS` (launcher.ts:47). The `AuthoringPlayId` union derives from it automatically.
3. Create packages/personas/base/plays/create-ticket.md mirroring create-priority.md: frontmatter `id: create-ticket`, `label: Create ticket`, `kind: headless`, `writeScope: [cocoder/tickets/**]` (covers the open/NNNN file AND INDEX.md). Body instructs the agent to: validate the invocation (title non-empty; type one of bug|task|question|spike defaulting task; priority defaulting none; owner = TICKET_OWNER; created = today); allocate the next id by the SAME rule as core `nextTicketId` (zero-padded NNNN, max existing across open+closed +1); slugify the title with the same rule the route uses; compose the file using the SAME core `composeTicketMarkdown` (the single format owner) so frontmatter id/title/type/status: Open/priority/owner/created is ALWAYS present — explicitly to prevent the 0015 silent-drop; write `cocoder/tickets/open/NNNN-slug.md`; insert the INDEX.md Open-table row in the EXACT route shape using the core `insertOpenTicketIndexRow` + `ticketTableCell` helpers (refuse on id collision); then VALIDATE the written ticket round-trips through core `loadTicket`/`readTickets` with NON-NULL metadata and matching id/title/type — fail loudly if not. End with: do not run git/commit; the dispatch harness commits the writeScope via the governance spine; leave only the intended ticket file + INDEX.md changed.
4. TESTS: (a) add a daemon authoring-play test in packages/daemon/tests/authoring-play.test.ts for create-ticket, mirroring the existing create-priority case — agent authoring writes cocoder/tickets/open/NNNN-*.md + an INDEX Open row, the file round-trips via readTickets with non-null metadata, and it commits through the spine; (b) add a core test that `composeTicketMarkdown` output parses via `loadTicket` with non-null metadata and correct fields. Do NOT weaken existing assertions; the daemon tickets-create test (route) must remain green to prove the extraction is behavior-preserving.

MUST NOT: add a second ticket-write path or a second owner of the ticket file format; change the founder-modal endpoint's external behavior; touch the dashboard UI; run git.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
1. `composeTicketMarkdown`/TICKET_OWNER live in @cocoder/core as the single format owner; routes.ts createTicket uses them; output is byte-identical (daemon tickets-create test green).
2. `create-ticket` is in AUTHORING_PLAY_IDS and packages/personas/base/plays/create-ticket.md exists, mirrors create-priority.md, templates full frontmatter, and validates round-trip.
3. New authoring-play test proves an agent-run create-ticket writes a valid, non-dropped ticket + INDEX row committed via the spine; new core test proves composed frontmatter round-trips.
4. `pnpm --dir '/Volumes/NAS LOCAL/CoCoder' typecheck` (root), `pnpm --dir '/Volumes/NAS LOCAL/CoCoder' --filter @cocoder/core test`, and `pnpm --dir '/Volumes/NAS LOCAL/CoCoder' --filter @cocoder/daemon test` all pass.

Report files changed, the create-ticket.md Play, the routes.ts extraction diff, and the typecheck/test output.
